# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!

  def custom
    @query = params[:query].to_s
    @result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(@query)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json do
        rows =
          if @result.respond_to?(:to_a)
            @result.to_a
          else
            Array(@result)
          end

        render json: {
          query: @query,
          row_count: rows.size,
          results: rows
        }
      end
      format.csv do
        rows =
          if @result.respond_to?(:to_a)
            @result.to_a
          else
            Array(@result)
          end

        headers["Content-Disposition"] = "attachment; filename=custom_report_#{Time.current.to_i}.csv"
        headers["Content-Type"] = "text/csv"

        keys = rows.first&.keys || []
        self.response_body = Enumerator.new do |y|
          y << CSV.generate_line(keys) if keys.any?
          rows.each do |row|
            y << CSV.generate_line(keys.map { |k| row[k] })
          end
        end
      end
    end
  rescue => e
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        flash.now[:alert] = e.message
        @result = []
        render :custom, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
      format.json do
        render json: { error: e.message, query: @query }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
      format.csv do
        render plain: e.message, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    end
  end
end
